concerns.text: T015 implements filtering for '<80% valid hours' but omits the '<90% task completion' threshold mandated by FR-001. This is a silent relaxation of the requirement, risking inclusion of low-quality data.

T016 describes 'MAD-based outlier removal' but omits the specific '3.5x' multiplier threshold defined in FR-003. Without this constant, the task deliverable is non-reproducible and may not satisfy the spec's statistical rigor.

T024 assumes a pre-binned categorical 'noise_level' column exists for Tukey HSD, but FR-002/FR-005 define noise as continuous ranges. No task explicitly creates this categorical column from the continuous bins, creating a data contract gap.

T025 applies multiple-comparison correction to the Likelihood Ratio Test (LRT) p-value alongside pairwise comparisons. FR-008 requires correction for '>1 hypothesis test', but LRT is a single model comparison, not a set of tests. This misapplication risks incorrect statistical inference.

T032 claims to implement FR-006 (threshold sweep), but FR-006 is about sweeping noise thresholds (handled by T031). T032 actually implements the 'exclude extreme sensitivity' logic from US-3, which lacks a dedicated FR. This is a traceability violation and potential requirement drift.

Phase 7 tasks pivot to finding a 'publicly available dataset' to replace the 'mobile app' assumption in the spec. No task exists to formally update the spec or document this deviation, violating Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth).
